title: Key Batch Records (01–10)
normalized_content:
phase_0_5b_batch_records_key_01_to_10.md
This will include:
● Batch V20012607002 (incoming event batch) – detailed.
● 9 other key historical batches across FPP-2001, FPP-1001, FPP-1003, and API-101, with
realistic interventions, deviations, and links to complaints/CAPAs.
Phase 0.5B – Key Batch Records (01–10)
Organization: Aetheris BioPharma Private Limited
Scope: Detailed batch manufacturing and packaging records for selected key batches
Date: 2026-07-11
Version: R0
All batch records below follow Aetheris numbering conventions and are consistent with
Phases 0.1–0.4, Master Index 0.5A, and Core SOPs 0.5B.
Batch Record 01
Document ID: VISK-BMR-FPP2001-2607-002
Product Code: FPP-2001
Product Name: Injectra 1 g
Batch Number: V20012607002
Site: VISK
Line: ML-ST-01
Manufacturing Dates: 2026-07-08 to 2026-07-09
Owner: M. Deepak
Related SOPs:
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0304-R0 (Vial Washing)
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0 (Vial Filling)
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0309-R0 (Line Clearance)
Related Equipment:
● EQ-ST-01-001 (Vial Washing)

● EQ-ST-01-002 (Vial Filling)
● EQ-UTL-002 (WFI)
Related Employees:
● EMP-0303 (M. Deepak) – Production Manager
● EMP-0417 (R. Chandra) – Fill Line Operator
● EMP-0418 (S. Fernandes) – Visual Support
● EMP-0204 (T. Srinivas) – EM Review
● EMP-0401 (J. Jose) – Engineering Support
Linked Events:
● Deviation: VISK-DEV-2026-0114
● Complaint: VISK-CMP-2026-0034
● Investigation: VISK-QA-RPT-2026-0048-R0
● CAPA: None yet (open event)
Status: On QA hold pending investigation closure.
1. Batch Synopsis
● Batch Size: 10,000 vials (nominal).
● Actual Yield: 9,968 vials after in-process rejects and segregations.
● Stages:
1. Vial washing (EQ-ST-01-001) – 2026-07-08.
2. Vial filling and partial stoppering (EQ-ST-01-002) – 2026-07-08 to 2026-07-09.
3. Secondary packaging (ML-PKG-02) – 2026-07-09.
Key Event:
During post-fill review and retained sample check, irregular crimp appearance and faint
whitish residue near stopper edge were observed on at least 2–3 vials. One tray (ST1-HLD-
27) was placed on hold. A distributor later reported similar visual findings in the market
(loose flip-off caps and powder-like ring).

2. Raw Material Issuance
Material Description Lot No. Supplier Qty Issued Status
Code
MAT- Injectra API (lyo API- Internal 10.2 kg Released
FP2001-001 powder) 1012604001 (PASH)
MAT-ST- Glass vials 10 SG-V- SUP-003 10,200 Released
003 mL 44721 units
MAT-ST- Sterile stoppers STP-77419 SUP-003 10,200 Released
004 units
MAT-ST- Flip-off caps, teal CAP-18802 SUP-003 10,200 Released
005 units
All materials issued per PASH-WH-SOP-WH-0401-R0 and VISK-WH-SOP-WH-0408-R0.
3. Vial Washing (EQ-ST-01-001)
Start: 2026-07-08 08:10
End: 2026-07-08 10:40
Parameters:
● Wash cycle: Standard Cycle C (VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0304-R0).
● Water: WFI from EQ-UTL-002.
● Final rinse conductivity: < 1.3 µS/cm (spec: ≤ 1.5).
In-Process Checks:
● Visual cleanliness: Pass (20 vials sampled).
● Endotoxin test on final rinse: < 0.05 EU/mL (spec: ≤ 0.25).

Deviation: None at this stage.
4. Vial Filling and Partial Stoppering (EQ-ST-01-002)
Start: 2026-07-08 11:30
End: 2026-07-09 13:55
Setup:
● Change parts: ST-CAP-B (per VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0).
● Fill volume target: 5.2 mL (nominal).
● Fill speed: 80 vials/min.
In-Process Checks:
Time Check Result Spec
11:45 Fill weight (n=10) 5.19–5.22 mL 5.15–5.25 mL
12:30 Fill weight (n=10) 5.18–5.23 mL 5.15–5.25 mL
13:15 Fill weight (n=10) 5.17–5.24 mL 5.15–5.25 mL
14:10 Visual check post cap-guide 1 uneven skirt noted –
adjustment
15:00 Fill weight (n=10) 5.18–5.23 mL 5.15–5.25 mL
Interventions:
● 14:10 – Cap feed guide adjusted due to intermittent cap seating variance (per machine log
VISK-ENG-LOG-2026-0179).
● 14:13 – Line resumed after visual check of 5 sample vials.
● 15:02 – Operator requested check on crimp profile sample; outcome “within visual
acceptability per prod.”
Segregation:

● End of run: 3 suspect vials identified during retained sample review (2 with uneven crimp
skirts, 1 with faint whitish ring near stopper edge).
● These were placed on tray ST1-HLD-27 and segregated.
Deviation:
● VISK-DEV-2026-0114 raised on 2026-07-10 (see investigation VISK-QA-RPT-2026-
0048-R0).
5. Lyophilization (Not Applicable for This Batch)
Injectra 1 g is supplied as sterile powder filled under aseptic conditions; no lyophilization
step for this product.
6. Secondary Packaging (ML-PKG-02)
Start: 2026-07-09 14:30
End: 2026-07-09 17:30
Process:
● Cartoning of 100 vials per carton.
● 100 cartons planned; 99 full cartons + partial carton completed.
Label Controls:
● Artwork: PW-IC-7714 (per CORP-RA-SOP-RA-0702-R0).
● Batch and expiry printed via thermal transfer.
● Line clearance performed per VISK-WH-SOP-WH-0408-R0.

Reconciliation:
● Labels issued: 10,200.
● Labels used: 9,968.
● Labels destroyed: 232 (damaged/misprints).
● Reconciliation: Balanced.
Observations:
● One shipper-case manifest screenshot later showed provisional dispatch reference ending
in 07003, causing confusion with batch identity.
● Actual physical shippers linked to V20012607002.
7. In-Process Quality Control
Test Sample Result Spec
Fill volume (IPC) 40 vials across 5.17–5.24 mL 5.15–5.25 mL
run
Visual inspection 100% 3 suspect units No critical defects
(100%) segregated
Container closure 10 vials Pass No leaks
integrity (CCI)
Particulate matter 20 vials 1 faint ring noted None visible
(visual) (seg.)
8. Deviation and Holds
● VISK-DEV-2026-0114: Irregular crimp appearance and possible stopper residue.
● Hold Actions:
○ Tray ST1-RET-09 (retained samples) – held.

○ Finished pallet FP-07 – held pending QA decision.
QA Disposition:
● Status: On hold pending investigation and complaint reconciliation.
9. Batch Record Review
● Production review: Completed by M. Deepak on 2026-07-09.
● QA review: Partial; final disposition page pending.
● EM trend summary: Not attached to initial packet (later provided as VISK-EM-2026-0088
and VISK-QC-RPT-2026-0281-R0).
10. Signatures
● Production Manager: M. Deepak – 2026-07-09 18:10.
● QA Reviewer: S. Balaji – review in progress.
● Final QA Release: Pending.
11. Attachments (Referenced)
● VISK-ENG-LOG-2026-0179 (machine log excerpt).
● VISK-DEV-2026-0114 (deviation report).
● VISK-QA-RPT-2026-0048-R0 (investigation).
● VISK-CMP-2026-0034 (complaint).
● VISK-EM-2026-0088 (EM excerpt).
● VISK-QC-RPT-2026-0276-R0 & ADD1 (lab results).
● Photograph descriptions: VISK-QA-RPT-2026-PH-0012.
● Witness statements: VISK-QA-FRM-2026-WS-0006 & ADD1.

Batch Record 02
Document ID: VISK-BMR-FPP2001-2506-002
Product Code: FPP-2001
Product Name: Injectra 1 g
Batch Number: V20012506002
Site: VISK
Line: ML-ST-01
Manufacturing Dates: 2025-06-15 to 2025-06-17
Owner: M. Deepak
Related SOPs:
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0309-R0
Related Equipment:
● EQ-ST-01-001, EQ-ST-01-002
Linked Events:
● Complaint: VISK-CMP-2025-0026 (visible cosmetic particulate in vial).
● Deviation: VISK-DEV-2025-0094 (Grade B viable air count at alert level).
● Investigation: VISK-QA-RPT-2025-0038-R0.
● CAPA: VISK-CAPA-2025-0028.
Status: Closed; batch released and distributed.
1. Synopsis
● Batch Size: 10,000 vials.
● Yield: 9,975 vials.
● Key Event: Post-market complaint of visible cosmetic particulate in one vial;
investigation linked to multi-factor causes including EM alert and cleaning sequence.
CAPA implemented for enhanced inspection and particulate trend review.

2. Key Process Parameters
● Vial washing and filling per VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0304-R0 and 0305-R0.
● Fill volume IPC: 5.18–5.23 mL (within spec).
● Visual inspection: 100% with 2 suspect units segregated.
3. Deviation and CAPA Linkage
● EM alert in Grade B corridor (4 cfu/m3) triggered deviation.
● Complaint received 2025-06-18 from UAE.
● CAPA VISK-CAPA-2025-0028 addressed inspection enhancement and trend review.
4. Disposition
● Released after investigation and CAPA initiation.
● No recall; complaint classified as isolated.
Batch Record 03
Document ID: PASH-BMR-FPP1001-2602-001
Product Code: FPP-1001
Product Name: Aethcard 10 mg
Batch Number: P10012602001
Site: PASH
Line: ML-OSD-01
Manufacturing Dates: 2026-02-08 to 2026-02-10
Owner: D. Anitha
Related SOPs:
● PASH-OSD-SOP-OSD-0205-R0 (Tablet Compression)
● PASH-OSD-SOP-OSD-0206-R0 (Coating)

Related Equipment:
● EQ-OSD-01-002 (TCM-01)
● EQ-OSD-01-003 (CP-01)
Linked Events:
● Complaint: PASH-CMP-2025-0048 (tablet breakage).
● Deviation: PASH-DEV-2025-0142 (compression force drift).
● CAPA: PASH-CAPA-2025-0037.
Status: Closed; batch released.
1. Synopsis
● Batch Size: 200,000 tablets.
● Yield: 199,450 tablets.
● Key Event: Compression force drift noted in a previous batch; this batch executed under
enhanced monitoring per CAPA.
2. Compression Parameters
● Target hardness: 8.5 kp.
● IPC hardness range: 8.3–8.7 kp.
● Hourly trend review performed; no drift observed.
3. Coating
● Weight gain: 2.8% (spec: 2.5–3.0%).
● Appearance: Uniform, no defects.

4. Disposition
● Released after successful review.
● Linked to PQR PASH-PQR-FPP1001-2025-001.

● Deviation: PASH-DEV-2026-0049 (printed component reconciliation variance).
● CAPA: PASH-CAPA-2026-0018.
Status: Closed; batch released.
1. Synopsis

● Batch Size: 300,000 tablets.
● Yield: 299,600 tablets.
● Key Event: Missing leaflet complaint in one retail carton; investigation identified leaflet
reconciliation gap.
2. Printed Component Control
● Leaflets issued: 300,500.
● Reconciliation variance: 20 leaflets unaccounted.
● CAPA enhanced reconciliation checks.
3. Disposition
● Released; CAPA implemented.
● Linked to PQR PASH-PQR-FPP1003-2025-001.
Batch Record 05
Document ID: PASH-BMR-API101-2604-001
Product Code: API-101
Product Name: Aetherol
Batch Number: API-1012604001
Site: PASH
Line: ML-API-01
Manufacturing Dates: 2026-04-03 to 2026-04-08
Owner: K. Ramesh
Related SOPs:
● PASH-API-SOP-API-0302-R0 (Reactor Charging)
● PASH-API-SOP-API-0303-R0 (Reactor Operation)
Related Equipment:

● EQ-API-01-001 (R-101)
Linked Events:
● Deviation: PASH-DEV-2025-0221 (reactor charging sequence not documented in real
time).
● CAPA: PASH-CAPA-2025-0049.
Status: Closed; batch released.
1. Synopsis
● Batch Size: 50 kg (theoretical).
● Yield: 49.6 kg.
● Key Event: Prior documentation weakness addressed via real-time recording
reinforcement.
2. Reaction Parameters
● Temperature profile: 25–65°C as per method.
● Agitation: 120 rpm.
● In-process IPC: Assay 98.9% (spec: 98.0–101.0%).
3. Disposition
● Released; used in multiple FPP batches including Injectra.

Status: Released; CAPA ongoing.

● Batch Size: 8,000 vials.
● Yield: 7,976 vials.
● Key Event: Lyophilization trend review and reconstitution performance enhancement

2. Lyophilization Cycle

● Secondary drying: 6 hours.
● Cake appearance: Uniform.
3. Dissolution Testing
● One market complaint of slower-than-expected dissolution.
● Investigation ongoing; no confirmed product-quality failure.
